sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.8 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$X$be a class of finite simple groups such that$( X) = char( X)$. A~formation of finite groups$F$is said to be$X$-saturated if a finite group$G$belongs to$F$whenever the factor group$G/ (O_{ X}(G))$is in$F$, where$O_{ X}(G)$is the largest normal subgroup of$G$whose composition factors are in$X$. Is every$X$-saturated formation$X$-local in the sense of F\"orster? (See the definition in (P.\,F\"o...
